title: Dedication to Geta as Caesar
support: Large limestone plaque, broken into three fragments, which have been reconnected. The lower portion of the plaque is lost. The plaque has a substantial plain moulding around the outer edge. The first line of text has been chiselled out.

provenanceFound._: Transported from a private house to the town hall by the time of Fazellus (1558), and long on display in the Palazzo delle Aquile, before being transferred to the museum, where it is now on display in the main courtyard

graphics.desc: I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo R. Henzel, 2016-11-07

museum.name: Museo Archeologico Regionale Antonino Salinas
museum.description: Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.

location.address: Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo
